title: Typed facts file
description: A domain-agnostic facts file for the deterministic lookup path. Each fact is a single atomic, citable value extracted verbatim from an authoritative source document. The LLM never generates these values — lookup answers are templated from this file, so a number cannot be hallucinated. The same schema works for any domain: efficiency standards, compliance thresholds, HR policy limits, contract terms, product specs.

domain.description: Free-text domain tag (e.g. 'hvac_efficiency_standards', 'process_safety', 'hr_policy'). Not an enum on purpose — the pattern is pluggable.

source_doc.description: Filename of the source PDF in data/documents/ (must match chunk metadata so citations line up with retrieval).

status.description: 'draft' = machine-assisted extraction, not yet human-verified. The API exposes this so a draft fact is never presented as authoritative.

parameter.description: What is being asked about (e.g. 'SEER2 minimum', 'threshold quantity').

entity.description: What the parameter applies to (e.g. a product class, a chemical, a policy).

qualifier.description: Optional disambiguator (e.g. region 'Southwest', capacity band). Facts that share parameter+entity but differ by qualifier trigger the clarify path when the query omits it.

condition.description: Optional applicability condition stated by the source (e.g. 'applies when SEER2 >= 15.2').

source_page.description: 1-based PDF page number — same convention as retrieval chunk metadata and the eval set.

quote.description: Verbatim supporting text from the source page. Every lookup answer shows this quote so the value is auditable.

keywords.description: Lowercase match terms for deterministic slot scoring.
